Off Calendar Jan-07-2014 Text Ruling

J. Niley Dorit, Esq. of Dorrit Law Firm [415.956.2757] appearing for Plaintiffs;
Dale L. Allen, Jr., Esq. of Allen Glaessner [415.697.2000] appearing for Defendant

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2013 Set For Trial On Sep-29-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Dec-18-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-28-2013 Continued To Dec-18-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Pltff'S First Cause Of Action
Off Calendar Oct-30-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE HIGHWAY AND TRANSPORTATION's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiff'S First Cause Of Action is Denied. The requests for judicial notice are granted. As to Government Code 815.6, Plaintiffs plead common carrier law as the statute or regulation giving rise to the mandatory duty. (First Amended Complaint ("FAC") 5.) This is sufficient. The Court will not consider the argument raised for the first time in reply that there is no common carrier liability as a matter of law. As to Government Code 815.4, the FAC at 2 pleads that defendants were "servants" acting within the "scope of their service." This is sufficient to state a cause of action against an independent contractor. As to 835, the FAC at 6 pleads that Defendant "built" and "maintained" the gates in an "unsafe and dangerous condition," which is sufficient to state a cause of action for premises liability. The Court also finds that the FAC is predicated on the same matters set forth in the claim submitted to Defendant pursuant to Government Code 910(f). The Court disregards the declarations filed in connection with this motion; the grounds for a motion for judgment on the pleadings "shall appear on the face of the challenged pleading or from any matter of which the court is required to take judicial notice." (CCP 438(d).) Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis , CSR # 12998 =(302/MJM)

Ntc Of Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Pltff'S First Cause Of Action
